summary: PASS on coder's post-security-v2 cleanup (commit 5157d10a). Diffs/Tags promoted to domain types symmetric with Audit/Trail/Errors/Children — security F1 closed at the source (no raw List<Diff> exposed, no race surface). F2 accepted with a documentation comment on the property where the torn-read materializes. The Flags rename (`CallStackFlags → App.CallStack.Flags`) and Debug.ParseCallStackFlags → Flags.Parse refactor are clean — zero residual `CallStackFlags` references in PLang, PLang.Tests, or Documentation. Tags becoming always-allocated has no broken consumer (no code null-checks Tags). Build green, 2623/2623 C# pass, 181/181 PLang pass after a clean PlangConsole rebuild. Two nits worth noting: Tags' explicit-interface-throws design is a documented Liskov compromise traded for DictionaryNavigator compatibility, and one PLang test goal carries a stale 'lazy-allocated' comment.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — v3 PASS verdict matches the code; the OBP refactor was clean and the rejected items have honest re-framings.
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — 181/181 reproduces here on a clean PlangConsole rebuild; the v1 stale-binary correction is the genuine root cause.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — v2's F1 recommendation was 'mirror the existing pattern across Diffs/Tags', which is exactly what 5157d10a delivers; F2 was accept-and-document and the document landed at CallStack/this.cs:30-35.

findings.issue: Tags implements `IDictionary<string,string>` but routes Add/Remove/Clear through explicit interface methods that throw NotSupportedException. Natural use (`tags.Set` / `tags["k"] = v`) works; a consumer who upcasts to `IDictionary<string,string>` and calls `Add` gets a runtime throw. This is a deliberate trade — IDictionary<,> is required for DictionaryNavigator's third arm to recognize the type for `%!callStack.Caller.Tags.foo%` PLang access — but it is a Liskov-substitution compromise.
findings.impact: Surface-area trap. No internal caller hits it (verified — all writes go through `target.Tag(key, value)` or `Tags.Set`); risk is future external code or a serializer that probes IDictionary<,>.Add. Doc on the type explains the rationale.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable as-is. If the trap ever materializes, alternatives are: (a) drop IDictionary<,> and enrich DictionaryNavigator to recognize IReadOnlyDictionary<string,T>; (b) keep IDictionary<,> Add as a no-throw alias for Set. Don't preempt — the doc is enough until something needs it.

findings.issue: Comment reads 'Tags dict is lazy-allocated.' Coder's 5157d10a flipped Tags to always-allocated (one-dict-per-Call) to close the writer-side race that lazy alloc introduced. The comment is now inaccurate; the test still passes because the assertion is shape-equivalent either way.
findings.impact: Doc drift inside the test goal. Future PLang author reading the comment learns a wrong fact about the runtime. Pure cosmetic; no runtime effect.
findings.suggestion: Update the comment to 'Tags is always allocated; %!callStack.Current.Caller.Tags% reads the caller's frame.' or just delete the lazy-alloc clause.

verifications.result: Zero hits in source/tests/docs. Only `.bot/` history references remain (frozen review artifacts).

verifications.what: Tags always-allocate has no broken consumer
verifications.how: grep for `Tags ?` / `Tags == null` / `Tags != null` in PLang and PLang.Tests
verifications.result: Zero hits. No consumer null-checked Tags.

verifications.what: Diffs domain class is symmetric with sibling collections
verifications.result: Same shape: private List<T> + private object _lock, IReadOnlyList<T> surface, snapshot enumeration. F1 raw-list exposure is gone.

verifications.what: F2 acceptance is documented at the relevant property
verifications.result: Lines 30-35 document the torn-read window and accepted impact (one off-by-one FIFO eviction).

verifications.how: Trace IDictionary<string,string> through DictionaryNavigator's three arms; spot-check that PLang test goals exercise the path
verifications.result: Third arm (GetStringKeyedDictType) matches IDictionary<string,string>. Tests/App/CallStack/TagBareLabelWritesTrue.test.goal and TagWritesPairsOntoCurrentCall.test.goal cover end-to-end. Both pass on clean rebuild.

verifications.what: Build + tests on clean state (don't trust prior runs)
verifications.how: rm -rf PlangConsole/{bin,obj} && dotnet build PlangConsole; dotnet run --project PLang.Tests; cd Tests && plang --test
verifications.result: C#: 2623/2623 (TUnit, 14.97s). PLang: 181/181 after clean rebuild. The pre-rebuild 5-failure run reproduces tester's exact stale-binary diagnostic — corroborates v1 correction.

carry_forward: Bounded-retention for Audit/Trail (security v1 F4) remains by-design open; future branch.
Sensitive-property stripping in FormatVerboseValue / AssertSnapshot (security carry-forwards) — pre-existing standing concerns, not this branch.
